reviews.review_msg: HE ZOOMS THROUGH EVERYTHING! I cannot keep up. Not recommended for people who are slow, like myself. If you enjoy a challenge, you might enjoy these lectures.

The guy is pretty good. He's not very organized and has terrible notes but he knows what he's doing. Just make sure you go over the material BEFORE the lectures, it'll be A LOT easier to follow him during lectures. Exams are fair, no surprises, as long as you do homework. Also, make sure you understand mixing, and volume problems for the final.
Not very good prof. Messy writing, so so teaching methods. BUT, tests very FAIR and EASY. Take him as a prof, then just teach yourself form text. Guaranteed good mark.

Wow! Decent prof. He keeps right on track even after being interrupted by conversations every 10 mins. He repeats vital theories and concepts throughout the semester. Just listen and do homework. Recommended.
Is dedicated to teaching the concepts, the only drawback is his notes are barely legible and jump all over the place.  Make sure to attend lecture!

Terrible Professor. His writing is crude and difficult to read. His explanations aren't clear, and his notes are all over the place. He often makes typos, then just scribbles it out, however sometimes the scribble still resembles a word, which leaves you asking, "What did he just write?". You can do better than Choksi.

This guy is probably one of those most dedicated profs at SFU. He will go out of his way to help his students-even when the students act like a**es to the guy. The difference between attending lecture or not will change your grade greatly-thats how good this guy teaches

An excellent professor. I like his philosophy on teaching: that it is most important to understand and learn logic more than memorizing formulas and data. He really takes the time to clearly explain concepts, and maintains a level of mutual respect with his students. As long as you respect him, he respects you, and cares for your success.

I was in the Prime section for Calculus 2, and I had Jamie Muholland and he is an amazing professor with great videos and great lectures. However, the tests were quite challenging, since I always loved Math and calculus since high school, I found the tests to be fair but they were quite conceptual and required a fairly deep understanding of math.
Jamie is one of the best profs at SFU. Would recommend taking any MATH course with him. Super organized and explains really well. Clear instructions and average tests.

Jamie Mulholland is a great prof for 152 . He is organized, caring, and has online videos. His videos are pretty good and allow you to go back on stuff at your own pace. Assignments and exams, are fair and marked on time.
Some of his explanations were so fire that I almost shed a tear, would take again!.
good teacher , but very hard exams

I left the class with a great understanding of the key concepts in calc 2. His lectures are useful and his pace is slow enough. Tests were fair and based on the homework questions. His accent was a little hard to understand at times.
He is funny and good at teaching. Tests are all easy. He is approachable but responds to emails very late and does not clearly answer questions and concerns students have most of the time.

Vijay is a down-to-earth guy, and definitely cracks a joke or two in lectures. I wasn't entirely fond of him writing his notes on paper, but he did spend time to rewrite them to look neater (though a bit late at times). A bit disorganized and marks are quite rigid, but he makes up for it by extending deadlines and correcting marking errors. 4.5/5

He is such a nice and easy-going guy. He is very open to questions and always finds a way to crack a joke. Although his notes were not really useful, and he writes a bit too fast and it does not help that he is left-handed when writing his paper notes, if you listen and just pay attention during lecture, you will be just fine.
Vijay is Awesome. Concepts are explained super well and always accessible for help in the course. Would highly recommend taking 152 with him.

Vijay is a great professor. He explains topics at a low level to the point that you won't come out of lectures confused. He is available out of class and provided us with sufficient material to prepare us for exams. He is generous with due dates and marking if you confront him during office hours. Don't skip class... He posts lecture notes late.

very good teacher, he goes into depth with topics and is always willing to emphasize if you don't understand something. doesn't make math unnecessarily boring or stale, and focuses on core topics without being too theoretical!
Goated prof, concepts were taught well and course was straightforward. He usually did reviews before midterms to help prepare for them. Really chill prof too.

Attendance is mandatory because he often posts his written lecture notes really late, even when the upcoming assignment is based on said notes. The weekly assignments are quite difficult and require a lot of digging around online to find answers but the midterms and the final are much easier. There are no trick questions on those tests.

I wasn't looking forward to taking MATH152 at first, as I didn't do so good in MATH150, however Vijay's lectures were extremely helpful; the exams were fairly straightforward as well. Vijay is very accessible outside of lecture, and he even made a discord server in the weeks leading up to the final exam so students could ask questions. GOATED prof

Vijay is a decent prof. He is very friendly and helpful, but he can sometimes be really careless. There are three non-cumulative midterms, and a fairly easy final as well. Honestly though, you can pretty much skip out on his lectures and do self-study or watch Jamie Mullholand videos. Not the best prof around, but he gets the job done pretty well.
Explains stuff pretty well. He made a discord server before the final and answered pretty quickly to math questions.

My love for math had slowly been dwindling. But, then Vijay started teaching me and rekindled a dying flame inside me. It was like I was watching one of those goated Indian YouTubers in real life. The dude seriously cares about his students and will go above and beyond to make sure you get what you deserve.

honestly hes a terrible prof, u will have to teach the material yourself. but, on the other hand, although i ended up getting an A in the course, my first A in university, thats because i got 85% on the final. highest ever. over half the questions on the final i saw in the notes or assignments. it was a very very easy final, easiest math final ever
very nice helpful prof. provide organized notes. eager to help students with hard questions. I aced.
horrible prof. avoid at all cost

Calc students, be warned... this prof might just have you running from the math dept.  Be prepared to teach yourself the hard examples on your own time - he can't be bothered to work them out himself.  As the term progresses, his energy is slowly drained and his exams become progressively harder.  wish he was rated on here before i took his class

Came into this course with a B+ from Calc 1. Didn't even need this course for anything so it was just for fun and out of interest. This horrible professor does not know how to teach. My GPA is taking a major hit. Don't take a course with him if you can't help it. You'll be left doing everything as an exercise.
Worst prof ever. Don't get stuck with him!
Awful. I don't think I've ever had such a bad prof and I've been in university for 4 years. Avoid at all costs!

Honestly, he's useless. He can't follow his own notes, tries to do the questions he posts in notes during class, and when he fails at doing them, leaves them as an "Exercise" (<-Catch phrase). If u have this prof, I suggest saving money and taking the course another term, you'll just end up failing and wasting your time.

The course should have been the same level of difficluty as Calculus I, if taught correctly. Not a good professor to have at ALL, he might be nice and yadd-dah, whatever. He still can;t teach, highly un-organized and very very poor notes.

Dr. Nessim changed my life, he's literally the best prof for MATH you can get at FIC / SFU. Idk why he got such bad reviews, but even though his lectures suck, but his exams are easy like a piece of cake. Take his class, buff your GPA up. Easy A+ if you try.

Joe knows the material well but sometimes explains the material in a manner that is slightly harder to understand for someone just beginning to learn the material. If you have any questions, he's willing to help and he does try his best to explain things.
difficulty of exams are fair, BUT this prof is useless. Come to lectures or not doesn't make any difference.

Joe is completely useless as a professor. He rarely ever explains the intentions behind what he is doing and constantly makes mistakes while doing it. If you see him as your professor, just avoid the class and take it with someone else. Jamie Mulholland is 100 times better.
Horrendous teacher can't understand jack about her online lectures, overcomplicates everything to the extreme and Randall is somehow better.
Doesn't know how to teach Calculus II, notes are very disorganized, she would spend time on easy questions and in the end just rush the hard ones, very inexperienced professor.
doesn't get any better than this

As the other ratings say, THE best professor in the entire school. His lectures are actually enjoyable to go to in spite of it being at 8:30AM. His clarity in teaching is the best of any professor I have ever seen. Yes, course material can be difficult at times, but I do not attribute it in the slightest to Dr. Mulholland's teaching ability.

If Jamie has one fan, it is me. If Jamie has ten fans, I am one of them. If Jamie has a thousand fans, I am one of them. If Jamie has a million fans, I am one of them. If Jamie has zero fans, then I am dead. If the world is against Jamie, then I am against the world. If Jamie has haters, I am none of them. I will love Jamie until my last breath.
Probably the BEST professor in SFU
Amazing prof, reasonable tests, make sures the test questions you have either seen before or will be able to do if u do all HW and study.
Jamie actually is the goat man, he explains everything so clearly and the midterms were pretty fair.
Best math prof you can find at SFU.
The best math prof you can find at SFU

Dr.M is by far the best teacher I have ever had across all subjects. His notes are unbelievably organized and well written, lectures are easy to understand and well paced, exams are fair and no trick questions. Had him for both Calc 1 and Calc 2 but he is even extremely knowledgeable on topics like linear algebra and discrete math too. 10/10

Dr. Mulholland is an amazing instructor and likely one of the very best professors at SFU. His lectures are so clear and he explains complicated concepts in a way that is easy to understand. I watched all of his videos for MATH 152 and attended all of his lectures in Spring 2024. His exams were very fair. Inspired me to do a math minor!
The GOAT of SFU Math Department.
He is a fun guy, note are simple and not very well organized though. Questions in exam are direct and easy. Just testing the basic concept. Need to remember theory definition.

A funny and likable guy. His accent may be difficult to get used to, but he tries his best to explain concepts clearly. He even took the time to go over assignments the day they were due. His final was really easy. :)
Funny  & doing his best. Cooperating and answers my questions very patiently that motivates me to learn.

Cory is such a dilligent professor and you can see it in her way of teaching and her moddle page. To do well in the course, just do all of the homework, it may look a lot but you can get it done if you just do like 5 question each day after the class which would take like 30 min - 1 hours it is all necessary since Cory hand picked these question.
The worst math 152 teacher in fic/sfu. You have to buy her book (if you dont print) and also buy the calculus 9 book. And her quiz has 2 different "code" and usually one is harder than the other.
I really wish I could met her earlier ^^ she's so good. You can always see the smile on her face.

Cory is kind and caring. Her tests are hard so I suggest you all study well. Also, do the homework as they may be useful for quizzes. You have to understand the materials deeply to get good scores in her class. Overall, she is a great mathematics teacher. I recommend her!

For the online classes she uploads videos with really good explanations. She gives a lot of homework everyday and it is crucial for u to complete them as they hold a lot of marks. She is very understanding and will give you and extension whenever you need it. I loved taking her class.

If you know your Math is not as good as the others, don't enroll in her class, because it's curved. If u got an iPad then no need to buy the material she made, she will post the pdf version. Practice the question from textbook is extremely important

I would say that she teaches in a way that is motivational for students. For example, she gives out bonus marks for those who would like to do her questions in the board. Also, I feel like she tries to interact with everyone by remembering our names and teach in a funny manner. If you study the way she teaches you, you will understand this course.

She is tough grader compared to instructor I took MATH151 with. But overall, if you write enough conditions for equations, do correctly, you, of course, will get full mark. Because she explains everything in detail, she lets students go home late. You would feel tired and bored at the end but it is good for students who know nothing about MATH 152

the pop extra credit quizzes are a godsend. punnen is a decent prof, however his lectures are just him stating facts instead of actually teaching. his heavy accent, mixed with his messy handwriting, mixed with the fact he makes lots of errors, makes it difficult to follow along, definitely use lots of outside lecture time to grasp concepts fully

Moved the weight of our lowest midterm to the final, exams are direct copies from assignments (if you do bad its on you). Really easy final (taken from assignments and midterms). Accommodates well for students, really cares about his students learning/wellbeing. Really caring prof, who knows how to teach, would recommend taking it with him. :)

He has is own textbook, but it's full with typos- it's annoying when you're practicing and you begin to question everything on there. he also has multiple select with multiple choice, which eats up a ton of time on the midterm. Grading scheme is also a pain if you're not good with exams- 90% exams, 10% homework. beware if you like to take your time
Can't understand him
Awesome prof.
This guys was absolutely boring.  He brought in Maple to every lecture and blabbed on and on about it even though we couldn't use it on the assignments or lectures.
She willingly wrote me a reference letter for my USRA project application - as a former student from her Calc 2 class, I definitely appreciated that
It's a shame that she doesn't teach that many classes. I hope the math dept reads these reviews and realizes that we NEED Lyn

The night before our final, she sent a message on the class wide Discord reminding us to eat and sleep properly. See? This is the difference: you have here a Prof who actually cares about the well-being of students.
She's got the weird mix of terrible handwriting yet amazing teaching. It's so hard to explain but I love it somehow
She got a standing ovation at the end of the semester when I took her Calc 2 in Spring of 2024

She definitely played a role in me taking more math courses later on. This class, though quite standard, definitely proved foundational for my later classes. She was brand new when I took her course (Spring 2024) and learning the ropes at first, but she improved significantly by the end of the course. Sounds like she's doing well now still!
You thought Calc 2 was this massive barrier of a course? Wrong! Lyn makes it accessible, fun, and you come out realizing the unthinkable: you enjoyed the course and want more.
Took her for Spring 2024, there were 3 midterms and a final. Very fair on the tests, practically like redoing the assignments in a timed setting. I don't know how you can get more fair than that.
Lyn was confirmation to me that any prof who uses Discord for their course is goated
Lyn is a great prof. tests were very fair and she actually cared about helping students understand.
Lectures are structured very well and easy to follow. She took the time to explain all the hard concepts if you're unfamiliar with them.

Lyn was an amazing professor. She never had any trick questions on exams. she was very approachable and even made a discord server to help students outside of class and office hours. It's obvious she cares for her students success and sadly that's not a common trait found in most uni professors.

Not bad. Her tests were fair, but I just did very poorly on them. One things is that some profs will give you one webassign a week with around 10-20 question on the material you learned, but she gives you 3 separate ones each with around 6-15 questions, which is more work weekly but good for final exam prep and knowledge overall.
Great professor.

Lyn was super great and super approachable during office hours. Calc 2 in general is a hard course but she made things easier to understand. The questions on the quizzes were from her homework and her test questions were fair. As long as you understand the notes and homework you'll do fine.

If you can take Calculus with her, I highly recommend it. Exams are very fair. Quizzes have same questions from assignments/ web assign questions, so also very fair. Stay consistent with practice every week, and you will do well. Lyn is very accessible and will always answer your questions without making you feel dumb. Goat of teaching calculus.
GOATED prof. I might have written a review here before lol. The classes and the discord server were godsend! Also the prof made a math class so fun and easy but yeah embrace the diy motto!

Great professor who cares about her students but it is a really hard course. Exams and quizzes are very fair, but still hard because this is calc 2. Student discord run by prof is very very helpful! Great all around prof!

Working through the offline and online assignmnents consistently helps alot as the weekly quizzes come straight from those! Moreover, the discord server both the unoffical and the unofficial one r helpful  Moreover, try brushing up precalculus + integration + trig + series and then calc 2 will surely be a breeze. Plus the prof is accessible!

Great prof, easy to approach, fair exams. Just make sure to do all the assignments by yourself and you are good to go. Also make use of the resources provided like office hours of prof and calculus workshop plus the discord server!!
Math requires a lot of work in general, but when Lyn is there, she will help you every step of the way
Calc II is a hard course but lyn is nice, teaches well, and has fair exams. Nothing more you could really ask. Calc II is just very hard lol
hard course but she makes it easy enough.
Very fair exams and quizzes, if you put in the effort.

I would say that she does not really know how to teach. Her Lecture notes are made to confuse students rather than helping them, so you have to study on your own. her tests are easier than what she gives in her lec notes, so that's nice.I would recommend taking this course right after CALC 1, since we use a lot of CALC 1 information in this class.
Good prof but the course is calc 2 so its pretty hard

Cant ask much more in a professor, shes very caring, wants to see you succeed, very fair exams and readily available to answer questions. If you have the chance to take her do it, you still have to put the work in cause the course is calc 2, but you will do fine with her.

Lyn is an amazing prof. She's one of the few that genuinely care about whether students succeed and understand the content. Her exams are fair, and she has plenty of office hours. She's new but honestly way better than most profs who have been here much longer. If you have the opportunity to take 152 with Lyn, I'd strongly recommend it.

She's about everything you would want in a Calc prof! Calc II is known to be tough, but Lyn makes it very manageable. Her lectures are clear, with examples that cover everything you need to know for the assignments. Exams are fair. She is also super approachable in office hours.
Great prof!

Lyn is one of the best professors I've had at SFU. Her lectures are very easy to follow and you can learn pretty much everything if you follow along with what she is teaching in the class. She has office hours that are very accessible and is also willing to do zoom meetings for extra help. She is super caring and genuinely wants you to succeed.

She is a very soft spoken person, so it's sometimes hard to hear her speak during lectures. Regardless, she is very good at simplifying complicated ideas down into Layman's terms, posts organized notes, and is approachable outside of class. Exams and assignments were very fair. Don't be lazy - put in the work outside of class and you'll do fine.

She is new to teaching but is already very good. Her lectures are pretty good; however, she sometimes skips steps but is willing to show them when asked. She consistently ran up to 5 min late every lecture. However, she had a ton of office hours for the final: 6 days and at least 10 hours total. She excels at explaining 1 on 1 in office hours.

Lyn improved drastically during the semester. I believe it was one of her first times teaching, so her lectures were a bit hard to follow at the beginning. This got much better throughout the semester, and she really took her time to make sure people understood before she moved on.

At the beginning it was a bit hard to understand her lecture,  but she got so much better half way through the semester. Super caring and really wants you to succeed. Before finals she had soo many office hours and had zoom meeting for help and explained everything so well with no judgment. Definitely recommend taking a class with her!
She's super helpful and nice. Considering that it's her first year teaching, she did a great job. I'd recommend anyone take a class with Lyn!
she's a pretty good prof overall and always gets right to the point when teaching

She's a super nice person and cares about students. She answers questions perfectly if you ask. Very approachable and gives many good suggestions for studying math. Due to personal issues, I had a very hard time at the beginning of the semester. She's very accommodating to my situation and willing to help me catch up. Exams are fair enough.

Lyn is really chill and explains most concepts nicely. She was a bit confusing at the beginning of the term, but has gotten better recently. She's willing to clarify anything that's unclear. Her class is quite organized and the exams are fair.

Honestly, she's not bad, especially since she is new. She does sometimes skip steps, but she's willing to explain them if you ask. Fairly accessible online and in office hours. Exams are very fair and based on the homework.

I understand that she is new at lecturing but I found it very difficult to follow what she was doing as she doesn't explain the steps thoroughly. I'm not a math whiz so take what I say with a grain of salt but I found that she doesn't show all steps and her writing is scribbly so its hard to go back and look at the posted notes and understand.

She's a great person but not the best lecturer unfortunately, really friendly and i'd suggest going up to her and asking if she can allocate office hours for you, because one on one she is actually pretty good. Personally, I wouldn't take her class again but i also understand she's new. Hope she gets better at this.
Test questions mostly taken from assignments and practice exams; do them and you'll be fine.
Really good prof

I love this man. Math is a weakness of mine. But his method of teaching just stuck with me and he made things make sense! I'm free from Calculus now but if I wasn't I'd be aiming to take every class taught by him.

I had to retake Calculus II because I received a D last semester. Professor Monagan is an amazing professor, and I couldn't recommend him enough. He is direct with what will be presented on the midterms and on the final, making it really easy to study for, and it's easy to achieve high grades. I ended this class with an A, all thanks to him.

Such a good professor. His lectures are extremely helpful as he doesn't use slides at all. Do all the assignments thoroughly and you will be all set to go, the exams are exact questions on assignments. No weekly quizzes either. If you are taking a calculus course take it with him!
one of the best math profs highly highly recommend him if you ever want a good grade in a math course!
One of the best math professors.
His lectures are very clear and the tests represent exactly what was taught

Best professor ever. I wish I had him for my MACM courses. He doesn't just spit out what's in his mind. He articulates the problem before teaching it and puts efforts in his notes. Yes his Notes, GOAT uses pen and paper to write clean, understandable and forever immortal notes

Although notes were made by another great professor, he explains the content clearly & gives funny, useful insight on it. Concepts are easy, but Calculus II is difficult unless you practice a lot! His exams are hard but agreeable. Eager to help, and humble too. Motivates us to do better on exams. With him, Math is fun. Beard is on point, btw!
Easiest Math 152 prof at SFU, obviously not an easy class but he makes it easy, chooses the easier questions for midterms and finals, great prof!
Very engaging prof
If you do the questions he assigns, you'll easily get a B. If you do more, its very easy to get an A+ in this class.

Super nice guy. Does surveys to find out when he should set up office hours. Always available to help. Gives you a note package at the beginning that you have to follow along and fill out. Tests are super easy if you follow his notes and do the homework
Funniest prof I've ever had. He made the class laugh every lecture. Generally easy-going, and tests are fair.

His test aren't incredibly easy and they're not hard. They are very straight forward and fair. He's a pretty good prof considering that it is his FIRST time teaching. Obviously to become a good teacher you need to gain experience by teaching. Also he's got a good sense of humor and he's lenient with grading.
he is a good guy but not a good prof. His teaching skills are terrible probably becuz it's his first time teaching. Exams are easy if you can study all by yourselves.

Very tough grader
Accessible out of class

A lot of self-reviewing. The material was hard for me, and she went through the lecture really fast, but it's not really her fault since there are only 50 min. But her explanations were really confusing sometimes.

Jessica is actually a good prof, even though she's new. She's very understanding even during a pandemic, and is clear and helpful when she teaches. I don't really understand why people are rating her really low, but she's a really good prof. However, the quizzes and exams are tough, but if you actually do the HW, you'll be fine

Definitely cares about success of students. Lectures are good, her notes are clear and she makes efforts to explain things in "plain english" which is appreciated. She is a reliable choice, I have no complaints.

Prof. Stockdale cares about her students, this is her first time teaching at SFU. Her notes are decent, though it's wordy, she tries to explain in the easiest way possible (in plain english) when she can. However, sometimes she does go a little overboard with the wordiness. Best is to keep it in short summarized (don't need perfect grammar)

A few people have written bad reviews for Professor Stockdale, but I think she's great. She explains the key concepts to you well and answers questions throughout the lecture (she doesn't make you feel stupid for asking). I've talked to her at the Calculus Workshop a few times and each time she is super open to answering my question. Overall great
She means well but is not that good at thoroughly explaining topics. I found myself watching videos that were shorter than lectures and learning more.

I find she does a really thorough job of going over the main points of the section's content in each lecture. That being said it's only a 50 minute lecture so she obviously can't cover every detail so the textbook is a useful resource. The course material itself is definitely quite challenging but I find her to be a very good teacher and very nice.

I've seen many of the reviews and people gave pretty bad reviews, but in my own opinion she makes calculus 2 not as bad as it seems. Though it's her first time teaching a math class shes not bad, she gives us most important material that we need to know. Quiz's are easy, it's just questions straight from the textbook/webassign, nothing too complex.
there is a difference between teaching and copying answers to blackboard, and she didn't show it.

Very unapproachable and belittles you if you don't know the answer or have the wrong answer to her questions in lecture. Quizzes and exams were tough with very little guidance on her part. Could've been a bit nicer - you don't have to speak to people like they're beneath you just because they're still learning what you're teaching. Avoid if you can

He is very good at explaining the material, but goes through it very quickly, so you need to concentrate very hard to get everything. Exams are very long and hard, but then he scales a lot. Questions in class are not very encouraged, but in office hours he is helpful. Rather good prof overall, but not easy.
Useless lectures, thick accent, and his exams are very difficult.

too many memes and chuck norris references his handwriting sucks and he goes way to fast for anybody to understand anything. dont take this unless youre doing it on its own or you really really love calculus
Teaches very fast and he's not very good at explaining concepts. His exams were difficult and lengthy. Avoid this teacher at all cost.
His exams had too many questions for a short amount of time. The exam questions were very hard. He also had a very thick accent and his writing was difficult to understand.
Nice guy, but thick accent makes it hard to understand what he's saying. Notes are barely legible, and often makes mistakes in his example problems.
Thick accent, bad writing, teaches too fast, not enough time for midterms.

Cant teach (tons of mistakes), really condescending & openly mocks students who received avg marks, NEVER gives enough time for quizzes (always only around 7 min instead of allotted 10), tests based on exercises from class which are the exact worked solutions from the book, never responds to e-mails, posts stupid memes constantly. AVOID!
DO NOT COUNT ON LECTURES. It is required you watch the lectures outside of class and in class he just gets confused and stares at the board as he tries to do his own questions.
All of the reviews matches. He tends to struggle doing problems when he has notes next to him. Biggest takeaway I got from Randall Pyke is that he looks like Chandler Lucy from The Hellp

Only giving this dude a 2 star rating because he at least shows up to class and try's to teach, but not rly. Bro can't do half of his own questions and his grading is extremely subjective on what is supposed to be one of the most objective subjects to grade.

Randall does not just casually make your life a living hell, he makes it his job to make sure it happens, which he only has time to do because he avoids his other job which is teaching, supposedly at least ,could have fooled me.
I regret taking this prof.
If you find yourself in a class with Big Pyke. I suggest you abandon everything, and go to the mountains and lay low for awhile. I am serious.
King Pyke is the best. Daddy

Everyone here is talking about the midterm which is fair it was absolutely horrendous, but can I just say this man cannot teach? He's barely managing to teach himself in class, while we watch hours of pre-recorded lectures from a different professor 4 years ago latest, this dude can't even manage to do the stuff he's attempting to teach.
save yourself dont take this course if you want to pass
You must watch lectures outside of class before actually going to his class.

Get Randall his meds. Absolutely no partial marks and if you don't do the question his way (which of course isn't specified in thr question) and you STILL get the right answer, that's really just a 0/5. Take calc 2 with literally anyone else.
rrrrrrrrrrrrrrun! ! ! ! ! ! ! ! ! !
This guy is absolutely goated, everyone needs a teacher like this in their lives, I feel as if I've found my purpose in life.
He does not care.
Randall is not good at teaching whatsoever.

Accent was difficult to understand, and the lectures were practically useless. Examinations and quizzes are fair, so if you do the textbook work and other practice assignments provided you will be good to go, but as a professsor don't expect much in terms of lectures, and be prepared to put in some work and teach yourself throguh the course.
accent is a bit tough to understand but amazing professor overall. fair/easy tests and curves crazy

The lectures were clear and the quizzes and exams were fair and in the same level as the things he covered in class so there was no surprising and hard questions. The sample midterms and finals were the most helpful.The grading was also fair. He helped me a lot during office hours patiently. Overall I'd say he's one of the best math profs I've had

Stephen Choi is a kind hearted guy who was always after class to answer all my dumb questions. Stephen Choi's accent is very hard to understand and his lectures are very hit or miss in terms of quality. I wouldn't recommend going to class and would suggest learning everything yourself to practice, practice, practice.

very mid. i dont like how he hides class averages. he seems like a nice guy, but he rambles on a lot and sometimes makes solving problems seem harder than what it is. the course structure (50% final) is also a huge downside.
he's very caring about students.. gives hints during quizzes. NO FORMULA SHEET THOUGH
